skill.name: writing-config-files
skill.description: Guides creation of small, modular configuration files for a Next.js app in src/config.
skill.summary: Create focused Next.js configuration files with one setting or helper per file.
skill.author: Emz1998

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution findings at SKILL.md lines 3 and 8 are false positives caused by Markdown inline code spans around src/config. The weak-cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is also a false positive; no cryptographic API, executable code, prompt injection, network access, or data exfiltration behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Spans Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution locations are Markdown inline code formatting in descriptive text. They do not invoke a shell or execute user-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only backticks appear in Markdown prose around a directory path. There is no Ruby file, shell script, command substitution, or executable context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm location is descriptive text about a Next.js configuration directory. No hash function, encryption function, or cryptographic library usage appears in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains skill metadata and a directory path reference. The file contains no cryptographic terminology or API usage.

content.value_statement: Next.js projects can become hard to maintain when configuration logic is scattered across large files. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create small config files in src/config with clear ownership.
actual_capabilities: Guides writing configuration files for a Next.js app under src/config.
Encourages one config function or variable per file.
Promotes splitting configuration into multiple focused files.
Sets a maximum target of 50 lines per config file.
Supports modular organization for maintainable app settings.
limitations: Does not define a specific schema for each configuration file.
Does not validate runtime behavior or environment variables.
Does not include examples for importing or exporting config modules.
Does not run tests or formatting commands by itself.

use_cases.title: Organize App Settings
use_cases.description: Create focused configuration modules for feature flags, routes, or UI constants in a Next.js project.

use_cases.title: Refactor Large Config Files
use_cases.description: Split a broad configuration file into smaller files with one function or value in each module.

use_cases.title: Set Team Conventions
use_cases.description: Apply consistent file size and module ownership rules when adding new project configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Config
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one new setting.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Next.js config file in src/config for one app setting. Keep the file small and focused.

prompt_templates.title: Split Related Settings
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for several related values.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create separate src/config files for these related settings. Use one function or variable per file and keep each file under 50 lines.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing Config
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request to improve existing organization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this existing configuration file and split it into modular files under src/config. Preserve behavior and explain the new file ownership.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Config Layout
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a growing app.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a src/config layout for a Next.js app with feature flags, routes, metadata, and external service settings. Keep modules focused and easy to import.

output_examples.input: Create configuration for public site metadata.
output_examples.output: A small metadata config module is added under src/config. It contains one exported value and stays within the file length rule.

output_examples.input: Split a combined settings file into smaller modules.
output: Feature flags are moved into a dedicated config file.
Route constants are moved into a separate config file.
Each new file has a single responsibility and a clear export.

output_examples.input: Plan configuration files for a new Next.js feature.
output_examples.output: A concise file plan lists the config modules to create, their purpose, and the single value or helper each file owns.

best_practices: Keep each config file focused on one function, value, or setting group.
Use clear file names that describe the configuration they contain.
Review imports after splitting files to avoid circular dependencies.
anti_patterns: Do not place unrelated settings in one large configuration file.
Do not hide runtime logic inside static configuration modules.
Do not duplicate the same setting across multiple config files.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create modular configuration files for a Next.js app inside src/config.

faq.question: Does it enforce a line limit?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs each config file to stay at a maximum of 50 lines.

faq.question: Can it split one config into many files?
faq.answer: Yes. It encourages separating configuration into multiple focused files when possible.

faq.question: Does it generate application logic?
faq.answer: No. It is intended for configuration files, not feature logic or component behavior.

faq.question: Does it run commands or install packages?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed skill file contains instructions only and does not run external commands.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.
